figures.caption: Table 13: Repair set size ablation (|P| fixed at 800 for SST-2, 200 for RTE).
Table 14: Remain set size ablation (|S| fixed at full size). ε∗med reported for DistilBERT only. Bold denotes the default setting.
Table 12: Rank ablation. SST-2: |S| = 108/105, |P| = 800. RTE: |S| = 47/50, |P| = 200. “Conv” denotes QP convergence iterations. ∗QP infeasible at rank 1.

Table 2: Dataset configurations. |S|: repair set; |P|: remain set; |Gen|: general accuracy evaluation set; |AtkGen|: attack generalization set. † Yelp Polarity test split (Zhang et al., 2015). ‡ RTE training split.
Table 9: Expanding-radius Monte Carlo stress test (tightness ratio: smallest multiplier k such that a flip is observed within k× ε∗).

Table 4: Main experimental results on SST-2 and RTE. “Repair Acc” is the accuracy on the repair set S ; “Remain Acc” on the remain set P ; “General Acc” on the held-out evaluation set; “Atk. Gen” is the mean accuracy across six adversarial attack methods (higher is better for all metrics). All sets are defined with respect to the post-GSN-FT weights; see §4.1.
Table 6: Effect of GSN-FT. Attack generalization is reported before and after GSN-FT to confirm that generalization gains originate from the QP step rather than preprocessing.
